mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes vs 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, with a hit rate below 40%, or the mechanism shows no directional edge when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation readings compress real interest rates and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures growth surpassing 2.5 percent year-over-year confirms robust consumer spending that lifts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered months, or the predictive accuracy does not significantly outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that raises wage costs and compresses profit margins, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings rebound, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 20-day returns in over 50% of post-shock recovery periods, or the signal information coefficient drops below zero when GDPC1 is negative.
